--- title: Insight through Data date: 5/31/2024 categories: - insights - python format: html: code-fold: false execute: freeze: true toc: true --- ## Crafting Visual Narratives for Corporate Strategy :::{.callout-important appearance="minimal"} All data in this case study is anonymized to protect confidentiality. Details have been altered, but the depicted processes and insights are accurately represented. ::: In my role as the Data Insights Specialist II on the Learning and Impact team at the Center for Employment Opportunities, I directed the overall strategy for each quarterly board report. I identified key performance indicators (KPIs) by analyzing data and aligning them with the strategic goals of the organization. I coordinated the narrative, deciding which insights to present, and shaped the story to highlight our achievements and areas for improvement. I'd communicate these insights through visualizations that were clear and impactful. Here, I'll share a glimpse into how I'd transform raw data [from various departments] into engaging visual stories that inform strategic and operational decisions. It’s all about turning complex data into clear, useful insights, even for those who aren’t familiar with data science or statistical jargon. Each quarter, I set these goals for myself: * **Show Org-Wide Performance:** Ensure strategic alignment with fiscal year objectives. * **Share Our Strategy for Improvement:** Highlight key initiatives and outcomes * **Delivering Actionable Insights:** Provide clear, impactful information to the board. ## Wrangling the Data At the end of each quarter, I'd reach out to various departments to gather updates on programs and activities. As the Data Insights Specialist II, I stayed informed on organizational activities year-round, as our team was heavily involved in evaluations and research. Proactivley engaging with pilots, program performance, evaluations, research, and analyses allowed me to know exactly what to ask for when compiling the board report, ensuring relevant insights were included. **The Data** Usually, these updates came back to me as rudimentary figures, spreadsheets, or dashboards from which I’d pull data points. I would then download these into .csv files and process them using my favorite tool: pandas. ::: {.grid} ::: {.g-col-4} ![Via G-chat: Quick messages containing key data points or updates.](the_data_1.webp) ::: ::: {.g-col-4} ![Through Google Sheets: More structured data in spreadsheets shared across departments.](the_data_2.webp) ::: ::: {.g-col-4} ![Inside Emails: Often, data came embedded within the text of an email, requiring careful extraction and verification.](the_data_3.webp) ::: ::: One quarter we included insights from an analysis we did on one of our intervention methods. Basically, we conducted a series of analyses to gain deeper understanding of what was driving our success. What came out of that was a set of characteristics, dosage ranges of interventions were increased the likelihood of success. Among ourselves on the Learning and Impact team and between departments we could easily talk about likelihood and probabilities especially when we're able to add more context but communicating probabilities and likelihood to board members on paper without the ability to dive into deeper conversation to tease out confusion is different. How does one visualizae clearly ranges and likelihood and dosages? My process for cleaning, summarizing and manipulating the results of these analyses is what I'd usually do to process data shared from different departments so I'll use this process as example for how I'd prepare data ppoints received for visualization. ::: {.cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} # Setting Up Environment import pandas as pd import numpy as np import matplotlib.pyplot as plt # Read in Data df = pd.read_csv("q3_program_data.csv") ``` ::: ::: {.cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} df.head() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=2} ```{=html}
Site Stage 1 Stage 2
0 Site 1 3 4
1 Site 2 5 6
2 Site 3 7 8
3 Site 4 9 10
4 Site 5 11 12
``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} df.dtypes ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=3} ``` Site object Stage 1 int64 Stage 2 int64 dtype: object ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code} df.columns = df.columns.str.lower() # df[[date]] = pd.to_datetime(df[date]) ``` ::: ## Designing Clear Visuals ## Final Product
